$comment: Caelum dependency-provenance allowlist — the machine-readable, checked-in source of truth the provenance gate (scripts/check_provenance.py) reconciles the resolved dependency tree against, and the US-origin attestation emitter (scripts/emit-us-origin-attestation.mjs) emits from. Derived from the M0-2 transitive provenance scan (docs/provenance/M0-2-transitive-provenance-scan.md) and ratified under D-11 (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md Appendix A). Adding a runtime dependency REQUIRES a row here or the gate fails — that is the forward-only ratchet (Book 03 §3.4, Book 12 §3.3). See provenance/README.md.

license.rule: Permissive + royalty-free, absolute, on every surface. Copyleft is rejected by default on BOTH the runtime and dev surfaces; a weak/file-level copyleft (MPL-2.0 / LGPL-*) is admissible ONLY as a never-shipped dev tool via an explicit devCopyleftExceptions row (Book 03 §3.1/§3.3, Book 16 §3.2). Strong copyleft (GPL) only by explicit human override; AGPL never; source-available (BUSL/SSPL/Elastic/PolyForm) never.

origin.rule: US-preferred; non-US avoided when a need can be met US-origin or self-built. Where it genuinely cannot, permissive-licensed deps from allied nations are acceptable. Angular's own transitive closure is accepted wholesale. Origin is by maintaining entity HQ, or — for individually-maintained permissive packages — maintainer residence (an individual US/allied resident under a permissive license satisfies origin; no US legal entity required).

policy.ssrStandingCondition: M0 posture is pinned to the zoneless browser client; SSR is out of scope. `entities` (GB) is the one non-US package that can reach a shipped bundle, and only on the SSR/server path. Adopting SSR is a POLICY TRIGGER requiring a human re-scan — NOT currently code-enforced (the gate has no SSR-path awareness; `entities` is already a vetted allied row, so an SSR build passes silently today): re-run the scan and either explicitly carve out `entities` or eliminate the parse5 SSR path before shipping an SSR build. A future gate enhancement could key off an `@angular/ssr` presence.

@angular/compiler.note: JIT only; AOT tree-shakes it out

@angular/forms.note: root dep; pulls zod + @standard-schema/spec (Angular's own closure — D-11)

@angular/cdk.note: root dep; per-entry-point; pulls parse5 on the schematics path

rxjs.maintainer: Ben Lesh / ReactiveX (Austin, TX — individual maintainer)
rxjs.note: peer of @angular/core; US by maintainer residence; no NOTICE file so Apache §4(d) not triggered

tslib.note: emit helpers inlined; 0BSD requires no attribution

zod.note: dep of @angular/forms; phantom/tree-shaken — absent from forms fesm2022; US by maintainer residence

parse5.note: ORIGIN FLAG (UK): dep of @angular/cdk; schematics/dev-tooling only, zero shipped browser bytes; Angular-brought-over; accepted under D-11

entities.note: ORIGIN FLAG (UK): dep of parse5; the one genuine shipped-runtime exposure but SSR/server-only, browser-inert; accepted under D-11 with the SSR re-scan standing condition

@standard-schema/spec.maintainer: Informal intl. working group (Colin McDonnell US, Fabian Hiller DE, David Blass)
@standard-schema/spec.note: ORIGIN FLAG (mixed/DE): dep of @angular/forms; types-only, erases at compile (dist/index.js is 0 bytes); accepted under D-11 (allied-nation fallback + Angular closure)

@tanstack/table-core.maintainer: Tanner Linsley / TanStack (Syracuse, UT — individual/community org)
@tanstack/table-core.note: framework-agnostic headless grid engine behind grid.adapter.ts (D-07, M2 grid adapter #171); dependency-free — no runtime deps, no peers (verified raw package.json 8.21.3, 2026-07-05); US by maintainer residence, mirrors the rxjs/zod precedent. Sign-off: docs/provenance/M2-grid-tanstack-signoff.md

@angular/animations.note: unmet optional peer of @angular/platform-browser; enters the shipped browser bundle the moment a consumer enables animations (provideAnimations()); pre-vetted US/MIT so an install passes the gate without a re-decision

zone.js.note: unmet optional dep of @angular/core; Caelum is zoneless-by-default so uninstalled, but a reachable shipped path if a consumer opts into zone-based CD; pre-vetted US/MIT

rollup-plugin-dts.reason: Weak/file-level copyleft build tool used by ng-packagr to bundle .d.ts declarations. Dev-only, contributes ZERO bytes to Caelum's published/browser bundle, and Caelum does not modify or redistribute its LGPL files — so the copyleft never reaches Caelum's distributed source. Ledgered per Book 16 §3.2 (the axe-core precedent) and Book 03 §3.1 (conditional dev-only admit).
